data.text: BUZZ-COMMENT-US recap: EUR/USD slips as dollar gets haven bid
data.snippet: The dollar rose broadly on Thursday, supported by a risk‑off tone stemming from Iran nuclear talks, weakness in tech shares after Nvidia’s earnings, and concerns over a rift between Anthropic and the Pentagon.

image.text: Euro and U.S. dollar banknotes are seen in this illustration taken May 4, 2025.

data.text: BUZZ-COMMENT-Echoes of the past are reason to seek shelter in currencies recently sold
data.snippet: Similarities between today and the global financial crisis suggest it may be wise to seek shelter in safer currencies —particularly the yen and the dollar, which have been heavily sold.

image.text: U.S. dollar, Euro, Chinese Yuan and Pound banknotes are seen in this illustration taken May 4, 2025.

data.text: BUZZ-COMMENT-Frustration for EUR/USD bulls is increasing
data.snippet: EUR/USD could extend its downtrend as investors' frustration grows amid softening U.S. interest rates and bearish technical signals.

image.text: U.S. dollar and Euro banknotes are seen in this illustration taken May 4, 2025.

data.text: BUZZ-COMMENT-Sterling lacks catalyst to break from well-defined ranges
data.snippet: Cable defined by narrow ranges in the current narrative vacuum. Though Thursday’s Gorton & Denton by-election will receive attention in the UK press, this is unlikely to be the case for markets.

image.text: Pound and euro banknotes are seen in this illustration taken January 6, 2020.

data.text: BUZZ-COMMENT-Dovish nominees should have little consequence for BoJ's near-term policy
data.snippet: A double hit for the yen in the last 24 hours has pushed USD/JPY through 156 and into rate-check territory. First, Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi signalled caution on further BoJ tightening.

image.text: Banknotes of Japanese yen are seen in this illustration picture taken September 22, 2022.

data.text: BUZZ-COMMENT-Reeves may put spring in sterling's step
data.snippet: The pound might elicit a boost if Tuesday's spring statement from UK finance minister Rachel Reeves conveys better than expected news about Britain's fiscal position.

image.text: Britain's Chancellor of the Exchequer Rachel Reeves takes a question from a journalist after speaking to nurses and members of the media during a visit to University College London Hospital in London, Britain, November 26, 2025.

data.snippet: Burned over the last week, yen buyers now appear reluctant. While USD/JPY slid on Thursday as both commodity prices and equities retreated, the pace of the decline was tempered by softer volatility and another benign U.S. weekly jobless claims print.

image.text: A passerby is reflected on an electric monitor displaying the graph of recent moments of the Japanese yen exchange rate against the U.S. dollar outside a brokerage in Tokyo, Japan May 2, 2023

data.text: BUZZ-COMMENT-Bullish yuan bets pay off as China eases property curbs
data.snippet: China's typically stable yuan has been on a tear, rising 2.1% against the USD since end-2025. More gains may be ahead, based on signals from the central bank and indications that Beijing is ready to act more forcefully to resuscitate the property market.

image.text: U.S. dollar and Chinese Yuan banknotes are seen in this illustration taken September 12, 2025.

data.text: BUZZ-COMMENT-AUD/USD: Respect for the bull trend, but gains are looking stretched
data.snippet: A seven-week bull run for AUD/USD has seen the Aussie trading at levels not seen since January 2023. But with weekly momentum indicators at levels not seen since March 2021 and the Relative Strength Index at a 17-year high, there may be signs of trend stress.

image.text: Australian dollars are seen in an illustration photo February 8, 2018.

data.text: BUZZ-COMMENT-OPEC may strongly influence the future path of the U.S. dollar
data.snippet: Eight OPEC+ members are set to meet on March 1, with the group leaning toward resuming oil‑output increases from April — a move that could weigh on the dollar, which fell sharply after the organisation began raising production in April 2025.

image.text: A view of the logo of the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries (OPEC) outside their headquarters in Vienna, Austria, November 30, 2023.

data.text: BUZZ-COMMENT-US dollar still struggling to recover from impact of 40-year old deal
data.snippet: The U.S. dollar is still struggling to recover from the impact of the 1985 Plaza Accord. After that agreement, the Dollar Index fell from about 165 to 85 by 1987 and eventually reached 71 in 2008.

image.text: A man walks past a foreign exchange house, with vinyl featuring dollar bills on its door and walls, in Ciudad Juarez, Mexico, February 10, 2026.
